match: Leeds United vs Burnley (Premier League, Matchday 35)
strat: Leeds United, currently 15th in the Premier League with 40 points, are highly motivated to secure their top-flight status. They enter this match unbeaten in their last five Premier League games, including a 2-2 draw against Bournemouth and a convincing 3-0 victory over Wolves. Despite a recent 1-0 loss to Chelsea in the FA Cup semi-final, their focus is now entirely on league survival. Under manager Daniel Farke, Leeds typically average 1.3 goals from 3.9 shots on target per game and maintain around 46% possession. Their home form at Elland Road is particularly strong, accounting for seven of their nine league wins this season, and they have shown improved defensive solidity, conceding only four goals in their last six matches across all competitions. Burnley, on the other hand, are 19th with 20 points and have already been relegated to the Championship. They are currently under the interim management of Mike Jackson following Scott Parker's departure. The team is in poor form, having lost their last four league outings, including a 1-0 defeat to Manchester City. Burnley has managed only one win in their last 25 Premier League fixtures, and their away record is particularly dismal, with six losses, three draws, and just one win in their last ten away games, conceding an average of 2.10 goals per match. Their attacking output on the road has also declined, averaging only 0.75 goals scored in their last eight away matches.
injuries: For Leeds United, Ilia Gruev is out for the remainder of the season due to a meniscus injury, and Gabriel Gudmundsson is sidelined for at least two games with a serious hamstring injury. There are also doubts surrounding the availability of Noah Okafor (calf knock) and Jaka Bijol (hip flexor problems), who will face late fitness tests. However, Anton Stach and Joe Rodon have recently returned to full fitness and are available for selection. Burnley faces a more extensive injury list. Captain Josh Cullen is a long-term absentee due to an ACL injury sustained in December. Jordan Beyer and Connor Roberts are also out with knee/hamstring and Achilles issues, respectively, with Roberts yet to feature this season. Hannibal Mejbri (thigh issue) and Axel Tuanzebe (Achilles problem) are doubts and will undergo late fitness checks. Zeki Amdouni, who had an ACL injury, has returned to training and might be involved before the season concludes.
ref: The referee for this match is Thomas Bramall. He is known for his officiating style, with an average of 4.15 bookings per match. The Video Assistant Referee (VAR) will be Tim Wood.
tip: Leeds United are strongly favored to win this match. Their urgent need for points to secure Premier League survival, combined with their solid home form and recent defensive improvements, provides a significant advantage. Burnley, already relegated and under interim management, is likely to lack the motivation and cohesion to challenge a determined Leeds side. Their poor away record and extensive injury list further diminish their chances. A comfortable home victory for Leeds is anticipated.
prob: The probability of a Leeds United win is 63.71%, with expected odds around 4/9 (-200). A draw has a probability of 20.57%, while a Burnley win is rated at 15.68%. The most likely scorelines for a Leeds victory are 1-0 (10.57%), 2-1 (10.26%), and 2-0 (10.23%). Given Burnley's tendency to concede goals away from home and Leeds' recent trend of higher-scoring games, betting on over 2.5 goals could also be a viable option.
